meaning: n.舷侧,较宽的一面adj.全面的adv.侧面地
examples: The ship was moored broadside to the pier.
The Cadillac slammed broadside into a Ford Escort.
The prime minister fired a broadside at his critics.
Washington Mutual "took the brunt of the broadside and was hammered ridiculously, " says Wright.
Amnon Rubinstein warned against this growing phenomenon in a broadside against Attorney-General Menachem Mazuz.
